title: Oldenlandia schimperi
taxonomy: Oldenlandia schimperi belongs to the family Rubiaceae. This plant is a species of flowering plant in the genus Oldenlandia. It is a herbaceous plant that is native to tropical and subtropical regions. The classification of Oldenlandia schimperi is further divided into the kingdom Plantae, and the order Gentianales. The classification of Oldenlandia schimperi is also a member of the class Magnoliopsida and the subclass Asteridae.

intro: Oldenlandia schimperi, also known as Schimperi's oldenlandia, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the family Rubiaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine, particularly in Ethiopia, for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to treat various ailments such as fever, cough, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Oldenlandia schimperi include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Oldenlandia schimperi include infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which are often used to treat respiratory and gastrointestinal disorders.
